hypothesis: Persisting checkout progress with clear recovery controls will improve completed purchases without increasing payment errors.
problem: Returning shoppers lose cart or checkout state after an interruption and must repeat work.
title: Resilient checkout and cart recovery

hypothesis: Improved query understanding and transparent result controls will raise search-to-cart conversion.
problem: Shoppers using descriptive or misspelled queries struggle to find relevant products quickly.
title: Intent-aware product discovery

hypothesis: Showing a trustworthy delivery promise earlier will reduce checkout abandonment and avoid expectation gaps.
problem: Delivery dates and fees are not sufficiently clear before checkout for time-sensitive purchases.
title: Delivery promise clarity

hypothesis: Simple benefits based on repeat behavior will increase retention without training customers to wait for discounts.
problem: Repeat shoppers cannot see a clear, relevant benefit for maintaining a DailyCart relationship.
title: Useful loyalty value

hypothesis: Consistent accessible controls and announcements will reduce blocked sessions and broaden successful task completion.
problem: Keyboard, screen-reader, and low-vision customers encounter inconsistent controls in key shopping flows.
title: Accessible shopping controls

hypothesis: The shipped foundation was expected to improve checkout completion rate while creating a baseline for later iteration.
problem: Earlier DailyCart behavior exposed a narrower version of this problem: Returning shoppers lose cart or checkout state after an interruption and must repeat work.
title: Historical foundation: Resilient checkout and cart recovery

hypothesis: The shipped foundation was expected to improve search-to-cart conversion while creating a baseline for later iteration.
problem: Earlier DailyCart behavior exposed a narrower version of this problem: Shoppers using descriptive or misspelled queries struggle to find relevant products quickly.
title: Historical foundation: Intent-aware product discovery

hypothesis: The shipped foundation was expected to improve shipping-step abandonment while creating a baseline for later iteration.
problem: Earlier DailyCart behavior exposed a narrower version of this problem: Delivery dates and fees are not sufficiently clear before checkout for time-sensitive purchases.
title: Historical foundation: Delivery promise clarity

hypothesis: The shipped foundation was expected to improve 90-day repeat purchase rate while creating a baseline for later iteration.
problem: Earlier DailyCart behavior exposed a narrower version of this problem: Repeat shoppers cannot see a clear, relevant benefit for maintaining a DailyCart relationship.
title: Historical foundation: Useful loyalty value

hypothesis: The shipped foundation was expected to improve accessible task completion while creating a baseline for later iteration.
problem: Earlier DailyCart behavior exposed a narrower version of this problem: Keyboard, screen-reader, and low-vision customers encounter inconsistent controls in key shopping flows.
title: Historical foundation: Accessible shopping controls
